_what: VERDICT 054 fixtures — Brineward band-2 necessity (idea-engine PROPOSAL 043). Every registered constant copied verbatim from the PROPOSAL 043 block (## PROPOSAL 043 . 2026-07-13T19:11:37Z . status: sim-ready) and its idea file ideas/gba-homebrew/brineward-band-necessity-2026-07-13.md @ idea-engine ea3744d. Committed game tables quoted verbatim from menno420/gba-homebrew @ 8bac80a70c82096828663d501af5f2790acbccc4 (games/brineward-nds/source/bw_sim.c + bw_sim.h). The runner reads ONLY this file — fully hermetic.

harvested_doc_lines_verbatim.progression_economy_sketch: Tier prices roughly triple per step so band-2 runs stay necessary, never optional.
harvested_doc_lines_verbatim.core_loop_loot: deep-water salvage is worth more per crate, so greed has a shape: one more fight or turn for home?

model.repair_cost_rule: ceil(missing_hull / BW_REPAIR_PER_GOLD), rounded against the player
model.banking: pier detour costing T_dock time + full repair paid from banked gold

policies.family: GRIND(b, m) for b in {0,1,2} x m in {1,2,3} plus GRIND-H(b, m) (bank early whenever hull <= theta after a won water); 18 policies
policies.descent: descend winning one water per band 0..b-1 scooping en route, pier-bank + repair at descent end; sinking restarts the descent
policies.loop: fight m waters in band b, pier-bank + repair; segments iid by full repair at every bank
policies.argmax_tie_rule: first in pinned order (implementation pin, disclosed)

decision_rule.evaluation_order: REJECT first, then APPROVE, then NULL
decision_rule.approve_central_argmax: neither GRIND(b*,1) nor GRIND(b*,3) — i.e. GRIND(b*,2) or a GRIND-H variant
decision_rule.approve_stability_conjunct: seed-20261310 stability leg reproduces the ruling through both twin evaluators

sensitivity_values.support_7pt_rule: {0, floor(D/2), floor(3D/4), D, D + ceil(D/4), D + ceil(D/2), 2*D}

implementation_pins_disclosed: 7-point support: the registration says 'quarter terms floored and disclosed' AND (gate F4) 'mean = D_b exactly on every band, axis point, and both supports'. All-floor breaks the exact mean for D not divisible by 4, so the sub-D terms are floored and their above-D mirrors carry the compensating ceil — {0, floor(D/2), floor(3D/4), D, D + ceil(D/4), D + ceil(D/2), 2D} — exactly the committed 5-point convention (floor below, D + ceil above); mean = D exactly for every integer D. The exact-mean F4 gate wins over a literal all-floor reading; disclosed here and in REPORT.md.

sensitivity-leg length and gate: the registration names seed 20261311 for sensitivity confirmations but pins neither leg length nor gate; pinned here to the stability convention (20,000 waters per leg, widened relative gate 1/20).
argmax ties broken by pinned policy order (first wins); no tie is expected on exact rationals.
GRIND-H threshold check runs after every won water, descent and loop alike; the descent-end bank happens regardless of the threshold on the last descent water (one bank, never two).

MC damage draws use Random.getrandbits(3) (5-pt, 8 equiprobable eighths mapped {1,2,2,2,1}) and Random.getrandbits(4) (7-pt, 16 sixteenths mapped {1,2,2,6,2,2,1}) — exact pmf, one draw per water, zero float thresholds anywhere.
MC time is accumulated in exact integer twentieths of a water-unit (all registered times are multiples of 1/20); G_S is an exact Fraction.
MC legs stop at exactly the pinned water count; a mid-segment stop leaves that segment's hold unbanked (truncation bias O(1/W), inside the gate).

SE headroom pre-check: asymptotic renewal-reward variance from the exact Arm-A enumeration — Var(R - G*T per renewal unit) / (N * E[T]^2) with N = waters_per_leg / E[waters per renewal unit]; compared to the gate squared in exact rationals; asserted >= 4 SE on every main leg, reported for the widened legs.

NULL axis operationalizations (checked in registration order): band-1-carries = argmax band 1 in >= 5 cells each with NEC0 >= 3/2; hold-cap-bind = argmax policy wastes crates (expected wasted > 0 per loop segment) in >= 5 cells; risk-straddle = a fixed T_dock column mixes reject-lean (NEC < 5/4) and approve-lean (NEC >= 3/2) across D0 (boundary D0-dagger named); dock-straddle = same across T_dock at fixed D0; margin-thin = any cell with 5/4 <= NEC < 3/2; arm-disagreement = stability twin-evaluator class differs from Arm A class; sensitivity-straddle = any sensitivity variant's cells-class differs from base.

SE headroom registration defect (found at implementation, disclosed): the registration claims the 1/50 main gate was 'pre-checked >= 4 SE headroom at the pinned leg length' — the exact renewal-reward variance from the Arm-A enumeration shows this is FALSE at 100,000 waters for the low-G legs (worst ~1.7 SE; the relative gate is narrow in absolute terms exactly where G is small). The headroom is therefore computed and REPORTED per leg (first-class anomaly where < 4 SE), while the registered run-invalidator stays the agreement gate itself. Direction stated: a headroom shortfall risks a FALSE breach of the confirmation gate, never a wrong decision number — Arm A exact rationals alone are decision-bearing.

Agreement-breach resolution: the registration says a MAIN-gate breach 'invalidates the run' (the clause sits in the Arm S main-leg sentence) AND registers 'arm disagreement' as NULL axis (vi). Resolved per the honest-null-explicit clause (every NULL axis is a finalized, citable finding, never a re-run request): any main-leg (1/50, seed 20261309) breach lands the verdict as NULL(arm-disagreement) with the breach legs cited. The stability leg's APPROVE conjunct is the registered CLASS reproduction — proposal text verbatim: 'AND the seed-20261310 stability leg reproduces the ruling', done-when: 'reproduces the ruling class through both twin evaluators' — a widened-gate (1/20) breach on a stability leg is a first-class anomaly and a dual-reading disclosure (results.json records the class under BOTH the registered reading and the stricter breach-denies reading), not a registered conjunct. Sensitivity-leg breaches are reported anomalies (reporting legs never flip). NOTE, disclosed for the audit trail: an earlier implementation draft carried the stricter breach-denies conjunct; it was conformed to the proposal's literal text after the first prototype run, and the stricter reading's class is permanently recorded alongside the ruling — the knife-edge is visible, not hidden.
